default: I'm here to support you. Could you tell me more about how you're feeling during this [PHASE] phase? Tracking your daily symptoms helps me give better advice.

responses.text: Cramps are common, especially in the [PHASE] phase. Heat pads, magnesium-rich foods, and gentle stretching can help soothe your muscles.
Feeling drained? During your [PHASE] phase, your body is working hard. Try a 20-minute power nap and focus on hydration.
Bloating usually peaks before or during the [PHASE] phase. Reducing salt and drinking dandelion tea or extra water can help flush out excess fluids.
Skin changes are tied to hormones in the [PHASE] phase. Progesterone can increase oil; try to keep your skin clean and stay hydrated.
Your metabolism shifts during the [PHASE] phase. It's natural to crave more! Try to balance treats with complex carbs like oats or nuts.
That's wonderful! You are likely feeling the 'high' of the [PHASE] phase. It's a great time for socializing and new projects!
Be gentle with yourself. Hormonal drops in the [PHASE] phase can affect your serotonin. Journaling or a short walk might help clear your mind.
Hormonal migraines often happen at the start of the [PHASE] phase. Rest in a dark room and ensure you're getting enough electrolytes.
Since you are near the [PHASE] phase, your fertility is likely at its peak. Look for 'egg-white' cervical mucus as a key sign.
In the [PHASE] phase, light movement is best if you're low on energy. Save high-intensity workouts for your Follicular phase!
Discharge changes throughout the month. In the [PHASE] phase, it's a sign of where you are in your cycle. Clear and stretchy means high fertility.
Breast tenderness is very common in the [PHASE] phase due to rising progesterone. A supportive bra and reducing caffeine can help.
Your sex drive often peaks during the [PHASE] phase (Ovulation). This is nature's way of encouraging reproduction!
A late period can be caused by stress, travel, or diet changes. If it's more than 7 days late, consider taking a test or seeing a doctor.
Light spotting can happen during the [PHASE] phase. It is often normal, but track if it happens every month.
Feeling queasy? Prostaglandins during the [PHASE] phase can affect the digestive system. Ginger tea is a great natural remedy.
Progesterone can raise your body temperature in the [PHASE] phase, making sleep difficult. Try keeping your room cooler at night.
Caffeine can sometimes worsen cramps or breast pain in the [PHASE] phase. Try switching to herbal tea for a few days.
You lose iron during the [PHASE] phase. Eating spinach, beans, or red meat can help keep your energy levels up.
High stress can actually delay your cycle. Take a moment for deep breathing; your wellness is the priority during the [PHASE] phase.
Hormones affect your gut too! High progesterone in the [PHASE] phase can slow things down, while prostaglandins can speed them up.
Hormones in the [PHASE] phase can change your scalp's oil production. You might find you need to wash your hair more often this week.
Alcohol can dehydrate you and make [PHASE] phase symptoms feel worse. Try to alternate each drink with a glass of water.
Magnesium is great for cramps, and Vitamin B6 can help with [PHASE] phase mood swings. Always consult a professional before starting new supplements.
